content: I'm thinking of sending a v2 for CondVar methods. If anyone still intends to reply on it, please let me know. I intend to send it on top of Boqun's "Makes CondVar::wait() an uninterruptible wait" patch.

Hmm.. seems we had a bug in the previous code?
https://github.com/Darksonn/linux/commit/3e225df52f611b8a3689fc3ea17426b67fb926ba#diff-555e995fb5cda24bb08b449fcbe225f12fb67812617879dc8f66058d1843560eL184

The last parameter of __wake_up should be a pointer instead of a u32
It's a pointer with flags in the lower bits.

Alice Ryhl said:

It's a pointer with flags in the lower bits.

I haven't found such a usage yet, do you know which ->func in wait_queue_entry_t, it's really used in that way?
Ok, so ep_poll_callback use the key in that way.
But I think for the general wait/wakup infrastructure, the key is just a parameter (like a thread parameter in create_kthread).
Ok, ,maybe Wedson wanted to use it as a flag for the binder case back then. But now what your patch does looks good to me. We can always re-introduce a parameter field whenever someone needs it.
I mean, I used it for the epoll stuff in v1 of the file abstractions.
Yeah, I remembered that now (but didn't notice it since I never got a chance to see the signature of __wake_up().)
And Wedson also had it in rust branch: https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/blame/rust/rust/kernel/sync/condvar.rs#L124
